title: TechForward Summit Preview: Who's Speaking and Why It Matters
body:
The TechForward Summit, set to open in Amsterdam on March 12, is shaping up to be one of the more substantive tech gatherings of the year. Organizers have assembled a lineup that spans AI, quantum computing, and energy infrastructure — a deliberate attempt, the conference director said, to force cross-domain conversation.

Headliners confirmed so far include Dr. Elena Vasquez, newly installed as CTO of Volta Systems, who is expected to discuss hardware modularity and the supply chain decisions behind the company's recent LuminCore acquisition. Also on the roster: Dr. Priya Sharma, whose work on AI-driven materials discovery has drawn attention from both the pharmaceutical and semiconductor industries.

The energy track will feature voices from utilities and grid operators, though specific names have not been confirmed. A spokesperson said at least one major U.S. energy company would be represented.

Panel topics include "the regulation gap in deep tech" — timely, given recent EU scrutiny of Volta's deal — and "when AI meets quantum," which is expected to draw a standing-room crowd.

Early registration data suggests attendance will exceed last year's by about 20 percent.
